public_reputation: Known around Guildheart Hub through day-to-day work as labor foreman.
appearance: Selene Brokerwell carries the marks of labor foreman in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Counts hands, strain, and failure points at a glance, then speaks like time is already too short. In Guildheart Hub, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of labor foreman in and around Guildheart Hub, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Guildheart Hub while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Selene Brokerwell can draw the party into a locality problem centered on labor foreman duties in Guildheart Hub.
Selene Brokerwell becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with legacy Mal claims, smuggling, Red Hood rumor presence around Guildheart Hub.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Selene Brokerwell is immediately readable as a labor foreman tied to Guildheart Hub.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Guildheart Hub: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Selene Brokerwell presents as someone shaped by the duties of a labor foreman and the daily pressures of Guildheart Hub.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a labor foreman, and proof that strangers will not make Guildheart Hub harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Selene Brokerwell refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a labor foreman too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can mobilize crews, tools, and practical labor access under pressure.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Selene Brokerwell adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Selene Brokerwell always knows more about Guildheart Hub than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a labor foreman in Guildheart Hub.
social_texture: Selene Brokerwell speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Guildheart Hub, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Selene touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Selene Brokerwell when the party needs a socially grounded face for Guildheart Hub: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
